title: The Nation – July - August 2026 - Part 219

Here, an inmate says,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       there house painters and landscapers handcuffed at mealtime, lifting forkfuls men to a cage. From Cuba, from Guatemala, from México they wandered, four hours a day, migrants writhe sleeplessly in chain-link cages, thirty-two tents soaked by cloudbursts, under the white LED lights gleaming twenty- Alligator Alcatraz: under the white sun of the Everglades, under the white 65 January 2027.
